preamble: The disappearance of the front open-mid vowel /ɛ/, typically through processes of syncope (internal loss) or apocope (final loss) as words evolve and stress patterns shift.

examples.note: Loss of final front vowels in various historical stages and dialects.
Vowel reduction and loss in hiatus or unstressed positions.

sources: Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction.
